fresh_facts.claim: US President Donald Trump renewed threats on April 9, 2026 of 'unprecedented force' against Iran if ceasefire terms are unmet, with all US ships, aircraft, and personnel with additional munitions remaining positioned around Iran.
A US-Iran ceasefire began on April 7, 2026 and was on day two as of April 9, 2026, with no confirmed strikes in Iran reported in the past day.
Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 10, 2026 in response to Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, which Iran labeled a ceasefire violation.
Israel ceased fire against Iran around 3:00 a.m. on April 8-9, 2026 per directives after final attacks, but remains on high alert for violations.
Israeli airstrikes targeted Hezbollah in Lebanon on April 10, 2026, with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu calling these operations separate from the ceasefire.
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte informed European allies on April 10, 2026 that the US expects deployments of warships or capabilities to the Strait of Hormuz soon.
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated on or before April 10, 2026 that ongoing negotiations with Iran are 'very real' and gaining strength.
The Pentagon spent $11.3 billion in the US-Iran conflict's first six days, with spending tapering recently as of April 10, 2026.
US forces conducted strikes on three Iranian nuclear facilities and Isfahan, while Israel struck an Iranian pharmaceutical plant, prior to April 10, 2026.
Iran retaliated with a missile strike on US Al Udeid base in Qatar on June 23 with no casualties reported.
The IRGC stated plans to attack US tech companies in the region amid retaliatory exchanges as of April 10, 2026.
Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face power plant obliteration as of April 10, 2026.
Former UK military commander Richard Kemp described the Iranian regime as 'terrified' and under 'immense pressure' after US elimination of multiple leaders over six weeks as of April 9, 2026.
High-stakes US-Iran talks led by VP JD Vance began April 10-11, 2026 in Islamabad, Pakistan to address nuclear issues, Hormuz reopening, and ceasefire terms.
European allies pressured for Lebanon inclusion in ceasefire negotiations, which Israel rejected as of April 10, 2026.
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei continued issuing rare public statements warning adversaries as of April 8, 2026.
Iran's government inner circle has entered a phase of acute paranoia, with Supreme Leader appearances expected to become increasingly rare and limited to carefully staged events as of April 8, 2026.
By late June 2025, more than 700 individuals had been arrested across various Iranian provinces with intensified security checkpoints, warrantless raids, and expanded surveillance.
Iran's economy remains in an unstable and vulnerable state following the 2025 Twelve-Day War, with economic shocks in the Strait of Hormuz region affecting global markets.
Prediction markets showed 8.5% odds of the Iranian regime falling by June 30, 2026 as of the reporting date.

regime_fall.current_status: A fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran entered its fourth day on April 11, 2026, but is rapidly deteriorating amid escalating tensions: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 10 in response to Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen it or face power plant destruction, and high-stakes US-Iran negotiations led by VP Vance began in Islamabad. The IRGC remains in de facto control with the new Supreme Leader issuing rare public statements, while internal paranoia and surveillance have intensified across Iranian provinces.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum on April 10, 2026 for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face obliteration of power plants, coinciding with Iran's closure of the strait in response to Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon and the start of high-stakes US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad.
